revision.text:
[[ja:祇園神社 (神戸市兵庫区)]] {{Refimprove|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Torii of Gion shrine.JPG | name=Gion Shrine | address=[[12-1 Kamigion-cho, Hyōgo Ward, Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture|兵庫県神戸市兵庫区上祇園町12-1]] | coordinates= {{Coord|34.693056|N|135.167222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities=[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime]] | established=Unknown | Rank=Village shrine | annual_festival=[[June 7]] | rituals=Summer Festival (July 13–20) | map={{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyōgo|label=Gion Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | prefecture={{ill|Hyōgo|qid=Q1078790|1=Hyōgo-ku, Kobe}} | deity={{ill|Kushinadahime|qid=Q505469|1=Kushinadahime}}; {{ill|Susanoo|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} }} [[File:Keidai of Gion shrine.JPG|thumb|Shrine grounds]] [[File:Haiden of Gion shrine.JPG|thumb|Central gate. The main hall is visible in the background.]] '''Gion Shrine''' (祇園神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Kamigion-cho|ja|上祇園町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigion-cho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|en|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|qid=Q1078790|lt=Hyōgo Ward|1=Hyōgo-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|qid=Q48320|lt=Kobe|1=Kobe}}, Hyōgo Prefecture. Situated on the slopes of {{ill|Gion-yama|ja|祇園山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion-yama|1=Gion-yama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q116459951}} north of {{ill|Okurayama Park|ja|大倉山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okurayama Park|1=Okurayama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16278260}}, the shrine is accessed via 88 steep stone steps. Locally known as "Hirano no Gion-san" (平野の祇園さん), it is believed to provide blessings for protection from water-related disasters, plague eradication, exorcism, and fulfillment of great wishes. The annual {{ill|Summer festival|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Matsuri|qq=}}, held over eight days in July, attracts numerous worshippers. == Deities == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛稲田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} Before the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} of the Meiji period, Susanoo-no-Mikoto was equated with {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|qid=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}. Nearby, place names such as Tennogawa, Tennobashi, Tennodani, and Tenno Onsen are thought to originate from connections to Gozu Tennō. == History == In {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|qid=Q3577278|lt=869|1=Zhenguan}} (Jōgan 11), when the {{ill|Kamogawa|en|Kamogawa|qid=Q31476|lt=Kamo River|1=Kamogawa}} in Kyoto overflowed, spreading disease, Susanoo-no-Mikoto, known as a deity who warded off epidemics, was enshrined at {{ill|Tōkō-ji|ja|東光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji|1=Tōkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11526024}} on Mt. Uryū in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|北白川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kitashirakawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11403873}}. The deity was transferred from {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|en|Hiromine Shrine|qid=Q11484399|lt=Hirohata Shrine|1=Hiromine Shrine}} in {{ill|Himeji|en|Himeji|qid=Q209801|lt=Himeji|1=Himeji}}. During this journey, a shrine was built in Hirano at the site where the deity's mikoshi rested overnight, marking the origin of Gion Shrine. As Susanoo-no-Mikoto was equated with Gozu Tennō, the guardian deity of {{ill|Jetavana|en|Jetavana|qid=Q305242|lt=Jetavana monastery|1=Jetavana}}, the shrine was also called Tennōsha. The divided spirit of Gozu Tennō enshrined at Tōkō-ji was later moved to the site now known as {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|qid=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}}. However, Yasaka Shrine denies claims that Hirohata Shrine is the main shrine of the Gion deity. == Amulets == * Striped {{ill|koyori|ja|こより|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/koyori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紙縒|1=koyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11605535}} charm of Somin Shōrai * Somin Shōrai straw charm * Somin Shōrai wooden hexagonal kokeshi-style charm {{ill|Somin Shōrai|en|Somin Shōrai|qid=Q7560421|lt=Somin Shōrai|1=Somin Shōrai}} amulets are deeply connected to {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|qid=Q11588425|lt=Gion faith|1=Gion Faith}} and are distributed by shrines and temples associated with this belief, such as Yasaka Shrine and {{ill|Shinano Kokubun-ji|en|Shinano Kokubun-ji|qid=Q10888022|lt=Shinano Kokubun-ji|1=Shinano Kokubun-ji}}. These charms often bear inscriptions like "Descendant of Somin Shōrai," and are believed to repel evil spirits and protect against disease. == festival == In addition to the annual festival on {{ill|June 7|en|June 7|qid=Q2616|lt=June 7|1=June 7}}, a summer festival (Gion Matsuri) is held from {{ill|July 13|en|July 13|qid=Q2687|lt=July 13|1=July 13}} to {{ill|July 20|en|July 20|qid=Q2728|lt=July 20|1=July 20}}, featuring numerous {{ill|yatai|ja|夜店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yatai|1=night shop|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11431250}} (food stalls) inside and around the shrine grounds. == Auxiliary Shrines == ; Kōtaisha : Enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}. The annual festival is on {{ill|October 17|en|October 17|qid=Q2962|lt=October 17|1=October 17}}. ; Kasuga Shrine : Enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} (one of the {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日三神|lt=Kasuga Sanjin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). The annual festival is on {{ill|March 13|en|March 13|qid=Q2400|lt=March 13|1=March 13}}. These two auxiliary shrines are located within the main enclosure ({{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|qid=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}}). ; Ichikishima-hime Shrine : Enshrines {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, a goddess associated with {{ill|Benzaiten|en|Benzaiten|qid=Q818468|qid=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten|1=Benzaiten}}. The annual festival is on {{ill|June 17|en|June 17|qid=Q2672|lt=June 17|1=June 17}}. ; Shiratama Inari Shrine : Enshrines {{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|qid=Q719665|lt=Inari Okami|1=Inari Ōkami}}. The annual festival is on {{ill|April 1|en|April 1|qid=Q2510|lt=April 1|1=April 1}}. ; Sarutahiko Shrine : Enshrines {{ill|Sarutahiko Okami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Okami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3090037}}, closely related to {{ill|Dōsojin|en|Dōsojin|qid=Q269515|lt=Dōsojin|1=Dōsojin}} and {{ill|Kōshin|lt=Kōshin|lt=Kōshin faith|ja|庚申講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 庚申待}}. The annual festival is on {{ill|October 11|en|October 11|qid=Q2927|lt=October 11|1=October 11}}. ; Karasuwara Shrine : Enshrines {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, and {{ill|Inari Okami|ja|稲荷大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Okami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神|1=Inari Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q719665}}. Originally located in Karasuwara Village upstream of the {{ill|Ishii River|ja|石井川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was relocated due to a dam project in 1905 (Meiji 38). It moved to {{ill|Himuro Village|ja|氷室村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Village|1=Himuro Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11549564}}, but suffered damage during the 1945 Kobe Air Raids and the 1995 Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake. In 2006 (Heisei 18), it was relocated to the grounds of Gion Shrine. The annual festival is on {{ill|November 15|en|November 15|qid=Q2982|lt=November 15|1=November 15}}. Additionally, a sacred stone called the "Ichigan Ishi" (One Wish Stone) is enshrined within the tamagaki. == Address == 12-1 Kamigion-cho, Hyōgo Ward, Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture == Transportation == * From {{ill|Kobe-Sannomiya Station|en|Kobe-Sannomiya Station|qid=Q377926|lt=Sannomiya Station|1=Kobe-Sannomiya Station}}, take Kobe City Bus No. 7 to the "Hirano" stop. == See Also == * {{ill|Hirano (Hyōgo Ward)|ja|平野 (兵庫区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano (Hyōgo Ward)|qq=}} * {{ill|Minatoyama Onsen|ja|湊山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minatoyama Onsen|qq=}} * {{ill|Hirano Shopping Street|ja|平野商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Shopping Street|1=Hirano Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q109361440}} == External Links == * [http://kobe-gionjinjya.com/index.html Gion Shrine Official Website] - Kobe Hirano }} {{translated page|ja|祇園神社 (神戸市兵庫区)|version=99005593|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:祇園神社 (神戸市兵庫区)]] [[ja:祇園神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11588444}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hyogo-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:History of Hyogo-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:Plague warding]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
